text: At 16:08:17, the SSH authentication succeeded for user "www-data" from IP 172.16.40.88 on port 55211. At 16:08:11, user "www-data" ran the 'find' utility as root via sudo. Running find as root is often used to locate sensitive files or SUID binaries for privilege escalation (T1548.003 (Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching)). At 16:08:21, user "www-data" used sudo to edit the sudoers file (/usr/bin/vim /etc/sudoers) as root. Modifying sudoers is a persistence and privilege escalation technique that can grant permanent root access (T1548.003 (Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching)). At 16:09:02, user "www-data" spawned an interactive root shell via sudo (/bin/bash). This grants full system control and is a strong indicator of post-exploitation activity (T1548.003 (Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching)). Overall, this sequence on host app01 represents a multi-stage attack: initial access via SSH followed by privilege escalation and persistence mechanisms, consistent with a hands-on-keyboard intrusion.
